steps.title: Require a hometown preference
steps.content: The claim is not merely that the narrator enjoys her hometown. It says she prefers her hometown to other places she has visited. The quotation must therefore show both sides of that comparison: experience traveling elsewhere and greater delight in the native town.

steps.title: Reject praise without comparison
steps.content: Choice A says there is always something fresh to discover, but it does not compare the hometown with other places. Choice B describes beautiful scenery around a village without stating a preference over travel elsewhere. Choice D praises the pleasure of becoming familiar with a region, but again gives no comparison. Choice C contrasts a life of journeying with never taking greater delight than in experiences within the native town. The answer is C.
